title: Dupatya bisumbellata

Within the family Apiaceae, Dupatya bisumbellata is classified under the subfamily Apioideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Apieae and the genus Dupatya. Dipsacus (Dipsacus fullonum) has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestive health.

The bioactive constituents of Dipsacus (Dipsacus fullonum) include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

The most common herbal preparations made with Dipsacus (Dipsacus fullonum) include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
